title: armeria alpina subsp. vasconica
intro: Armeria alpina subsp. vasconica, also known as the mountain thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its pink or white flowers and tufted growth habit. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The mountain thrift is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of Armeria alpina subsp. vasconica, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
